requestedDecision: Adversarially review the planned approach for rigor issue #23 before implementation: ship the versioned test-integrity promotion framework (promotion criteria with explicit numerator/denominator evidence requirements, stop/review/advisory enforcement levels, policy/schema version binding, human waiver evidence, replay/shadow comparison gate) with an EMPTY default promoted set, because the recorded shadow dataset (4 events, TI-05..TI-09 evaluated=4, fired=0, humanClassified=0, outcomes=0) empirically qualifies zero signals. Identify: (1) whether this vacuous-satisfaction reading of the acceptance criteria is defensible or an improper end-run around the issue's human-approval prerequisite; (2) design flaws in the anti-bypass requirement (enforcement must not be disabled by editing promotion/signal config or deleting checks in the same unapproved change); (3) schema/evidence-integrity risks (pooling across detector-version strata, unattested human declarations, waiver misuse); (4) anything in the contract acceptance criteria that is untestable or self-contradictory. Separate facts, options, a recommendation, uncertainty, and decisions that must remain human.
requiredActions: Keep active promotion state in a specifically protected registry outside .rigor/evidence/** (which codePaths excludes), compare it independently across pinned base/head commits, and execute the anti-bypass decision with a verifier the evaluated change cannot replace.

Scope all claims to structural consistency of cited unsigned records plus external protected review for activation/waiver authority; never claim the evaluator proves empirical provenance or human identity.

Add an event-level per-signal evaluation manifest (detector name/version, candidate-set version, configuration digest) for future events; treat the four existing non-firing denominators as unversioned and ineligible for version-bound promotion.

Define separate metrics (firing rate, review coverage, precision/false-discovery proportion, uncertainty treatment, labeled non-firing audit sample for false negatives); do not call falsePositive/evaluated a conventional false-positive rate.
Label any shipped numeric criteria as human-approved conservative governance risk floors, not empirically calibrated values, and record the human decision amending the docs' no-threshold stance.

Replace absolute append-only claims with append-on-create plus base/head tamper detection; reject duplicate logical identities, preserve tombstones, rely on protected Git history for durable audit semantics.

Define a canonical proposal digest excluding activation/replay linkage; replay the immutable proposal against immutable committed inputs; create a separate activation referencing both; express before-enablement ordering via Git ancestry or external review state, not createdAt.

Make rollback conditions trigger a fail-closed freeze/review state requiring a separately protected activation change to deactivate; bind every waiver to one outcome digest, signal occurrence, promotion version, repository state, scope, reason, expiry, and external review.

Specify the full protected subject for anti-bypass (trusted bundle/source-tree digest and canonical registry paths) and bind evaluation to pinned base/head SHAs with a recheck immediately before the recorded outcome.

Do not use current retrospective totals as promotion authority: the promotion gate must strictly validate schemas and linkage, reject duplicate logical IDs and contradictory classifications, verify cited event digests, and report malformed evidence as promotion-ineligible.
Mark fixture provenance explicitly and make production promotion validation reject synthetic/test evidence; retain synthetic data solely for deterministic tests.
Separate signal disposition, enforcement effect, and recorded outcome in terminology; define advisory as a zero-gating-effect recorded outcome.

Rewrite absolute claims (necessarily cites, cannot be bypassed, exactly as before) as testable invariants over a named trust model: strict citation/link validation, differential golden tests with no active records, base/head tamper cases, explicit reliance on protected GitHub controls.
